topic: RevDeck plugin SDK and specialized reverse-engineering expansion
strategy: Ship a local, adapter-first plugin SDK preview before marketplace or broad custom UI/plugin APIs.

features.summary: Machine-validated plugin manifest declaring identity, compatibility, capabilities, permissions, inputs, outputs, UI slots, config schema, determinism, and provenance.
Stable SDK schemas and host-mediated ObjectBatch APIs for objects, edges, typed attributes, datasets, analysis runs, evidence links, diagnostics, scores, and draft findings.
Process-based importer/adapter contracts for Ghidra/rizin exports, traces, crashes, firmware trees, pcap/tshark, Volatility JSON, and generic JSONL evidence.
Declarative host-owned lens, inspector, command, cockpit, and query extension points for Trace, Crash, Firmware, Graph, Diff, Protocol, and Memory labs.
Explainable scoring/rule-pack SDK where plugins emit provider-attributed score reasons with confidence, evidence refs, suppression state, and deterministic merge behavior.
Least-privilege process runtime with filesystem, network, process, artifact, project-write, secrets, timeout, output-limit, and redaction scopes recorded per plugin run.
SDK scaffolding, manifest validator, headless runner, fixture replay, deterministic digest checks, sandbox tests, and TUI/CLI contract fixtures.
Plugin suggestion workflow for draft findings, evidence chains, report fragments, redaction flags, and analyst-confirmed report exports.

exit_criteria: process plugin runner records audited runs
rule pack reasons visible in Function Radar
